claim: No Stable, performance-scale, release, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made.

semantics.nativeBasis: A named role=tree owns roving role=treeitem descendants with level, position, set size, expansion, disabled, busy, and optional selection state.
semantics.multiselect: Multiple mode exposes aria-multiselectable and modifier-free selection; none mode emits no selected semantics.

interaction.keyboard: Up, Down, Home, End, mirrored logical expansion/collapse, parent/child movement, star sibling expansion, typeahead, Space/Enter selection, Ctrl+A multiselect, and optional F2 rename are supported.
interaction.lazy: Expanding an unresolved child may call the consumer loader and exposes bounded busy/error recovery state.
interaction.virtualization: A consumer-controlled window preserves real set positions and requests a new window when active focus crosses it.
interaction.touch: Expansion and optional non-drag move actions have explicit target-sized controls.

enhancementIsolation.moveActionsFalse: No move group, buttons, names, callback, or move event path exists.
enhancementIsolation.renameOmitted: F2 is not prevented and no input or rename callback exists.
enhancementIsolation.loaderOmitted: No load callback, busy status, error status, or load announcement exists.
enhancementIsolation.virtualFalse: All visible treeitems render and no spacers or range callback exist.

preferences.forcedColors: Tree boundary, selection, active seam, errors, controls, and focus use system colors.
preferences.rtl: Expansion and collapse keys mirror with logical indentation.

limitations: Source-present-unreleased records do not include generated parity, packed consumers, performance-scale proof, or manual assistive-technology evidence.
Virtual window ownership, durable lazy data, move authorization, and persistence remain consumer responsibilities.
